name: Babka (Festive Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the milk in a small saucepan until bubbles form around the edge. Remove from heat and let cool.
2. In a large bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water (105°F-115°F). Stir in lukewarm milk, sugar, salt, butter, eggs, egg yolk, and first measure of flour. Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth and well blended.
3. Using a wooden spoon, stir in the second measure of flour. Beat vigorously until the dough leaves the side of the bowl (about 2 minutes). Mix in raisins.
4. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk (about 1 hour).
5. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 9" springform pan. Turn dough into prepared pan and cover with a towel. Let rise in a warm (85°F) place until the dough is about 1/2" from the top of the pan (about 1 hour).
6. Prepare topping: Beat egg white with water and brush over babka.
7. Mix flour, sugar, and cinnamon. Sprinkle on top of babka.
8. Bake until a cake tester inserted in center comes out clean (about 60 minutes). Cool in pan on wire rack for 15 minutes. Remove sides and bottom of springform pan. Cut into wedges. Serve warm.
